pattern: Graph two-coloring on the dislike relationship: if every connected component can be colored with two colors so that no dislike edge connects same-colored people, a valid split exists.
hints: Build an undirected graph where an edge between two people means they dislike each other, then ask whether the graph is two-colorable.
Use BFS or DFS to color each unvisited person, assigning the opposite color to each neighbor; any conflict means two people in the same group dislike each other.
Process every connected component independently; if any component fails the two-coloring check, return false, otherwise return true.

edge_inputs.description: four people with dislikes forming a bipartite structure
three people mutually disliking each other form an odd cycle impossible to split
five people with dislikes that still permit a valid partition
single person no dislikes always splittable
two people who dislike each other trivially splits into one each
odd cycle of length five cannot be two-colored
no dislikes at all everyone can go in one group
two separate components one of which is an odd cycle forces false

stress.description: random graphs built from a planted bipartite structure occasionally with an odd cycle injected
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    side = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n + 1)]
    seen = set()
    dislikes = []
    # add cross-side edges to bias toward bipartite
    for _ in range(min(n * 2, 200)):
        a = rng.randint(1, n)
        b = rng.randint(1, n)
        if a == b:
            continue
        if side[a] == side[b]:
            continue
        key = (min(a, b), max(a, b))
        if key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(key)
        dislikes.append([min(a, b), max(a, b)])
    # occasionally inject a same-side edge creating an odd structure
    if n >= 3 and rng.random() < 0.4:
        same = [(a, b) for a in range(1, n+1) for b in range(a+1, n+1) if side[a] == side[b]]
        if same:
            a, b = rng.choice(same[:min(len(same), 100)])
            key = (a, b)
            if key not in seen:
                seen.add(key)
                dislikes.append([a, b])
    return [n, dislikes]
stress.note: mixes clearly bipartite inputs with deliberately non-bipartite ones
